what_it_does: Presents a generated Markdown tree for the Design DNA, organizing laws, spaces, circuit, faces, instruments, proofs, and method with addresses and references to docs, JSON faces, and code files.
observations: Declares itself generated from dna/canon.json with rebuild and lint commands.
Includes an attribution statement naming Tim Geldard as author of the principles, laws, and theories.
Uses seven top-level sections, each with a fractional span address.
Defines fourteen law nodes, four space nodes, two circuit nodes, eighteen face nodes, eighteen instrument nodes, one proofs section, and two method nodes.
Most child nodes include references using faces, docs, or code lines.
Frames the system around derivation, fit, resolution, evidence classes, addresses, and merge portability.
standouts: The document treats the canon as a computable tree with span addresses rather than only prose documentation.
The laws sector is described as portable across project merges, while faces are described as identity-specific registries.
The resolution model explicitly separates cascade from compose and records a retracted earlier framing.
The instruments section binds documentation concepts to concrete app, surface, runtime, server, build, and graph files.
conventions: Generated-file warning appears at the top and maintenance instructions appear at the bottom.
Top-level sections use numbered headings and span notation.
Child nodes use dotted identifiers and fractional address notation.
References are grouped under arrow-prefixed lines for docs, faces, and code.
Descriptions combine doctrine-like statements with implementation pointers.
Some entries use lowercase opening text while most use sentence-style capitalization.
concerns: Several entries contain dense multi-clause claims that may be hard to scan in a generated reference file.
Capitalization and phrasing are inconsistent in a few node descriptions, especially near faces.types and faces.roles.
The file gives rebuild and lint commands but does not show generated timestamp, source revision, or build status.
notes: Brief someone that this is the generated navigational tree tying the Design DNA principles to registries, documentation, proofs, and working surfaces.
questions: From this file alone, what exact schema does dna/canon.json use to produce these sections?
From this file alone, which entries are required versus descriptive?
From this file alone, how are the fractional addresses validated or consumed?
From this file alone, what distinguishes a face from an instrument when both point to runtime files?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it is a generated single-tree projection of the Design DNA with addresses, summaries, and references.
apparent_themes: artifact as fit between subject, telling, structure, and reader
intention_signals: It seems intended to make a design system legible as an addressable, generated, linted tree that links conceptual laws to registries, documentation, proofs, and implementation surfaces.
novelty: The file combines design principles, generated structural addresses, evidence language, proof obligations, and code references in one tree-shaped ledger.
contribution: It contributes an addressable Design DNA map with portable laws, identity registries, implementation surfaces, proof references, and method notes that a fused system could retain as a structured interpretive and navigational layer.
summary_for_embedding: Generated CANON.md is a seven-sector Markdown projection of the Counterpart Design DNA tree, linking laws, spaces, circuit concepts, registries, instruments, proofs, and method to addresses and file references.
intention_for_embedding: This file was made to serve as the generated, linted reading and navigation surface for the Design DNA so principles, registries, tooling, and proofs remain tied together.
